label: Indicative with ὅτι
rationale: All 3 tokens from expansion_patch.tsv (Phase 3.9) retained, none dropped. "object" is the most frequency-discounted (df=27, 2.6%) since it also legitimately appears across many unrelated Object-Complement-cluster nodes; this is exactly the term that caused the original Phase 3.8 ambiguous-expansion-failure tie, so discounting it here is intentional and targeted.

label: Epexegetical
rationale: Both tokens from expansion_patch.tsv retained. Lower secondary-slot usage than cs255 reflects that "Epexegetical" recurs legitimately at 4 ontology locations (Phase 3.6 finding) and does not need additional broad coverage.

label: Indirect Discourse (under Participle root)
rationale: All 5 distinct tokens from the original Phase 3.9 patch text ("verb of perception; perception verb; supplementary participle; accusative plus participle") are retained -- an earlier draft of this re-tiering dropped "verb" and "participle", which caused a genuine recall regression (see disambiguation_rules.tsv row 8 for the catch and fix). This node is now at its 5/5 secondary cap: any future addition here must either replace an existing term or move into the remaining 3 contextual slots.

label: Temporal
rationale: time is deliberately demoted from secondary to contextual tier specifically because of its high document frequency (df=23/1032=2.2%, shared with 22 other nodes including the literal "Genitive/Nominative/Dative of Time" case categories) -- this is the direct fix for the reported "cs378: time系ノードの過剰ヒット(45件)" complaint. "duration"/"chronology" stay at secondary tier since both have df=0 (unique to this patch, no collision risk).
